title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her former patient, Giovanni Thompson.
Giovanni 'Gio' Thompson, who had Morquio syndrome, was a patient of Donnelly's at Franciscan Children's for six months.
Gio's mantra 'I can do hard things' inspired Donnelly; he passed away six months after leaving the hospital.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Boston Bruins lost 4-3 to New Jersey Devils in overtime, affecting their playoff position in the Eastern Conference.
Jack Hughes assisted three goals, reaching 402 career points; Paul Cotter scored the OT winner for the Devils.
David Pastrnak scored twice for Boston, but the team failed to secure back-to-back victories since late January.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, from Tyngsborough, charged with murder after remains found under his garage linked to a missing Lowell woman.
The remains believed to be Jill Kloppenburg, 47, missing since January 2025, were reported by a friend of Sullivan.
Police used radar technology to find and recover human remains beneath a patched area of Sullivan's garage floor.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman from Houston is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise money for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Her son, Charles, has Dup15q syndrome and has been treated by Dr. Ronald Thibert at MGH for epilepsy.
Felterman honors her son and Dr. Thibert, crediting MGH for exceptional care and support in her son's epilepsy journey.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers from Belmont is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his wife, Coney Flowers.
Coney Flowers was diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia in 2024 and underwent successful treatment.
Ben is raising funds for Caring for a Cure, an organization that supported them during Coney's treatment.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie, 33, from Southampton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass General's Pediatric Cancer team.
Labrie adopts 'I Run For Them' motto to honor pediatric cancer warriors, survivors, and angels with each run.
Donors can choose honorees to support in Labrie's marathon run, expanding the 'I Run For Them' community.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Out of Body' will be available on Netflix starting April 13.
Kahan's fourth studio album, 'The Great Divide,' is set to release on April 24.
The documentary covers Kahan's fame, mental health, and Vermont roots during his 'Stick Season' success.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' after being laid off last fall.
Keller's 'Keller At Large' show will resume this Sunday, featuring Gov. Maura Healey as the guest.
Keller, a 12-time Emmy winner, will continue his MASSterList column and contribute to Boston magazine.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts Senate to review bill eliminating biannual clock changes, moving to permanent Atlantic standard time.
Boston.com poll shows 83% of readers support ending clock changes, citing disruptions and health risks.
Concerns include darker winter mornings and regional coordination, requiring other New England states' participation.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham was arrested for driving 118 mph and fleeing police in New Hampshire.
The teenager crashed into a retaining wall in Stratham after failing to navigate a traffic island, authorities said.
He faces multiple charges, including felony reckless conduct and aggravated DUI, and is held at Rockingham County Jail.
